speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Can no man tell me of my unthrifty son?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: ’Tis full three months since I did see him last.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If any plague hang over us, ’tis he.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I would to God, my lords, he might be found.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Inquire at London, ’mongst the taverns there,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For there, they say, he daily doth frequent

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With unrestrainèd loose companions,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Even such, they say, as stand in narrow lanes

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And beat our watch and rob our passengers,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: While he, young wanton and effeminate boy,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Takes on the point of honor to support

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So dissolute a crew.

speaker: PERCY
text: My lord, some two days since I saw the Prince,

speaker: PERCY
text: And told him of those triumphs held at Oxford.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And what said the gallant?

speaker: PERCY
text: His answer was, he would unto the stews,

speaker: PERCY
text: And from the common’st creature pluck a glove

speaker: PERCY
text: And wear it as a favor, and with that

speaker: PERCY
text: He would unhorse the lustiest challenger.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As dissolute as desperate. Yet through both

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I see some sparks of better hope, which elder years

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: May happily bring forth. But who comes here?

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Where is the King?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What means our cousin, that he stares and looks so wildly?

speaker: AUMERLE
text: God save your Grace. I do beseech your Majesty

speaker: AUMERLE
text: To have some conference with your Grace alone.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Withdraw yourselves, and leave us here alone.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What is the matter with our cousin now?

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Forever may my knees grow to the earth,

speaker: AUMERLE
text: My tongue cleave to my roof within my mouth,

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Unless a pardon ere I rise or speak.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Intended or committed was this fault?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If on the first, how heinous e’er it be,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To win thy after-love I pardon thee.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Then give me leave that I may turn the key

speaker: AUMERLE
text: That no man enter till my tale be done.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Have thy desire.

speaker: YORK
text: My liege, beware! Look to thyself!

speaker: YORK
text: Thou hast a traitor in thy presence there.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Villain, I’ll make thee safe.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Stay thy revengeful hand. Thou hast no cause to fear.

speaker: YORK
text: Open the door, secure, foolhardy king!

speaker: YORK
text: Shall I for love speak treason to thy face?

speaker: YORK
text: Open the door, or I will break it open.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What is the matter, uncle? Speak.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Recover breath. Tell us how near is danger

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That we may arm us to encounter it.

speaker: YORK
text: Peruse this writing here, and thou shalt know

speaker: YORK
text: The treason that my haste forbids me show.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Remember, as thou read’st, thy promise passed.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: I do repent me. Read not my name there.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: My heart is not confederate with my hand.

speaker: YORK
text: It was, villain, ere thy hand did set it down.—

speaker: YORK
text: I tore it from the traitor’s bosom, king.

speaker: YORK
text: Fear, and not love, begets his penitence.

speaker: YORK
text: Forget to pity him, lest thy pity prove

speaker: YORK
text: A serpent that will sting thee to the heart.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O heinous, strong, and bold conspiracy!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O loyal father of a treacherous son,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thou sheer, immaculate, and silver fountain

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: From whence this stream, through muddy passages,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Hath held his current and defiled himself,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thy overflow of good converts to bad,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And thy abundant goodness shall excuse

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: This deadly blot in thy digressing son.

speaker: YORK
text: So shall my virtue be his vice’s bawd,

speaker: YORK
text: And he shall spend mine honor with his shame,

speaker: YORK
text: As thriftless sons their scraping fathers’ gold.

speaker: YORK
text: Mine honor lives when his dishonor dies,

speaker: YORK
text: Or my shamed life in his dishonor lies.

speaker: YORK
text: Thou kill’st me in his life: giving him breath,

speaker: YORK
text: The traitor lives, the true man’s put to death.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What ho, my liege! For God’s sake, let me in!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What shrill-voiced suppliant makes this eager cry?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: A woman and thy aunt, great king. ’Tis I.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Speak with me, pity me. Open the door!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: A beggar begs that never begged before.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Our scene is altered from a serious thing

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And now changed to “The Beggar and the King.”—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My dangerous cousin, let your mother in.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I know she is come to pray for your foul sin.

speaker: YORK
text: If thou do pardon whosoever pray,

speaker: YORK
text: More sins for this forgiveness prosper may.

speaker: YORK
text: This festered joint cut off, the rest rest sound.

speaker: YORK
text: This let alone will all the rest confound.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: O king, believe not this hard-hearted man.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Love loving not itself, none other can.

speaker: YORK
text: Thou frantic woman, what dost thou make here?

speaker: YORK
text: Shall thy old dugs once more a traitor rear?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Sweet York, be patient.—Hear me, gentle liege.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Rise up, good aunt.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Not yet, I thee beseech.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Forever will I walk upon my knees

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And never see day that the happy sees,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Till thou give joy, until thou bid me joy

speaker: DUCHESS
text: By pardoning Rutland, my transgressing boy.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Unto my mother’s prayers I bend my knee.

speaker: YORK
text: Against them both my true joints bended be.

speaker: YORK
text: Ill mayst thou thrive if thou grant any grace.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Pleads he in earnest? Look upon his face.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: His eyes do drop no tears, his prayers are in jest;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: His words come from his mouth, ours from our breast.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: He prays but faintly and would be denied.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: We pray with heart and soul and all beside.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: His weary joints would gladly rise, I know.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Our knees still kneel till to the ground they grow.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: His prayers are full of false hypocrisy,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Ours of true zeal and deep integrity.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Our prayers do outpray his. Then let them have

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That mercy which true prayer ought to have.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Good aunt, stand up.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Nay, do not say “stand up.”

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Say “pardon” first and afterwards “stand up.”

speaker: DUCHESS
text: An if I were thy nurse, thy tongue to teach,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: “Pardon” should be the first word of thy speech.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I never longed to hear a word till now.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Say “pardon,” king; let pity teach thee how.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: The word is short, but not so short as sweet.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: No word like “pardon” for kings’ mouths so meet.

speaker: YORK
text: Speak it in French, king. Say “pardonne moy.”

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Dost thou teach pardon pardon to destroy?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Ah, my sour husband, my hard-hearted lord,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That sets the word itself against the word!

speaker: DUCHESS
text:
To
                 
                King
                 
                Henry
                .
                 
              Speak “pardon” as ’tis current in our land;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: The chopping French we do not understand.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thine eye begins to speak; set thy tongue there,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Or in thy piteous heart plant thou thine ear,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That, hearing how our plaints and prayers do pierce,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Pity may move thee “pardon” to rehearse.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Good aunt, stand up.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I do not sue to stand.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Pardon is all the suit I have in hand.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I pardon him, as God shall pardon me.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: O, happy vantage of a kneeling knee!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Yet am I sick for fear. Speak it again.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Twice saying “pardon” doth not pardon twain,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: But makes one pardon strong.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I pardon him with all my heart.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: A god on Earth thou art.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But for our trusty brother-in-law and the Abbot,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With all the rest of that consorted crew,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Destruction straight shall dog them at the heels.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Good uncle, help to order several powers

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To Oxford or where’er these traitors are.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: They shall not live within this world, I swear,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But I will have them, if I once know where.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Uncle, farewell,—and cousin, adieu.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Your mother well hath prayed; and prove you true.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Come, my old son. I pray God make thee new.
